claim: Context is the single most critical factor for agentic coding tools; generic prompts (e.g., 'build me a good application') fail because the AI lacks domain knowledge about the target protocol.
actionable: Before using Claude Code or similar agents to build DeFi applications, provide extensive context: protocol documentation, contract interfaces, deployment addresses, and specific use cases.
source_title: Building AI Agents on Bonzo Finance

claim: Documentation and contract addresses should be indexed and easily accessible in one place; this enables AI agents to quickly locate and interact with the correct smart contracts.
actionable: Maintain a centralized, up-to-date documentation site listing all smart contract addresses, ABIs, and interfaces; reference this in agent prompts.
source_title: Building AI Agents on Bonzo Finance

claim: Forking battle-tested protocols (like Aave V2 or Beefy Finance) reduces security risk and development time compared to building from scratch, even if customization is required.
actionable: When building DeFi protocols, consider forking audited, widely-used protocols as a base rather than building from scratch; plan for necessary customizations.
source_title: Building AI Agents on Bonzo Finance

claim: When building AI agents that interact with DeFi vaults, understanding the underlying strategy architecture (vault contracts → strategy contracts → external protocols) is essential for proper integration.
actionable: Document the architecture of vault systems explicitly: describe how vault contracts delegate to strategies, and how strategies interact with external protocols like DEXs and lending markets.
source_title: Building AI Agents on Bonzo Finance

claim: Single-asset vaults using the ICHI protocol allow users to supply liquidity with only one token while the vault handles rebalancing in the background—simplifying UX but adding complexity for agent interaction.
actionable: When building agents for single-asset vaults, account for the fact that the vault internally converts assets; communicate this in prompts to avoid agent misunderstanding of capital flows.
source_title: Building AI Agents on Bonzo Finance

claim: Custom vault interfaces (non-ERC-4626) are common in production; agents must understand that deposit() takes assets while withdraw() takes shares, creating asymmetric function signatures.
actionable: When writing agent prompts for custom vaults, explicitly state the asymmetry: deposits use assets, withdrawals use share quantities; this prevents logic errors.
source_title: Building AI Agents on Bonzo Finance

claim: Concentrated liquidity management (CLM) with main and alt positions reduces impermanent loss compared to constant rebalancing; positions stay in a narrow range and rebalance only on large price moves.
actionable: When designing agents for concentrated liquidity strategies, understand that positions should stay in a configured range and rebalance infrequently—not constantly.
source_title: Building AI Agents on Bonzo Finance

claim: Keeper and harvester bots can be built to earn fees by auto-compounding vault yields; low gas fees on certain chains make this economically viable even for small harvest amounts.
actionable: Build autonomous harvester agents that periodically call vault harvest() functions; on low-fee chains, this generates compounding returns from small fee captures.
source_title: Building AI Agents on Bonzo Finance

claim: Intelligent rebalancing agents should monitor external market signals (oracle feeds, volatility, market depth) rather than only internal vault metrics when deciding rebalance timing.
actionable: Design agent decision logic to pull external market data (price feeds, TVL trends, volatility indices) in addition to vault health; feed these signals into rebalance decisions.
source_title: Building AI Agents on Bonzo Finance

claim: AI agents interacting with crypto should always have strict guardrails: transaction limits, daily spend caps, and multi-signature approvals to prevent catastrophic failures.
actionable: Never grant agents unrestricted access to wallets or contracts; implement per-transaction limits (e.g., max $100/tx), daily caps (e.g., max $500/day), and require human review for large actions.
source_title: Building AI Agents on Bonzo Finance

claim: DCA (Dollar Cost Averaging) strategies across multiple vaults can be automated; agents monitor and allocate capital incrementally across vaults based on configurable rules.
actionable: Build agents that implement dollar-cost averaging across multiple vaults: split a target investment amount across multiple vaults on a schedule (daily/weekly) to reduce timing risk.
source_title: Building AI Agents on Bonzo Finance
